{{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)}} [[Category:Shortest 100 pages]] [[Category:as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:Translated shrines and temples]] [[Category:11]] {{Shrine |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命}} |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[ja:王子神社]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Completion Tier 1]] to [[Category:completion tier 2]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

{{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)}} [[Category:Shortest 100 pages]] [[Category:as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:Translated shrines and temples]] [[Category:11]] {{Shrine |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命}} |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[ja:王子神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)}} {{Shrine |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命}} |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shortest 100 pages]] [[Category:as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:Translated shrines and temples]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:王子神社]] [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]]

For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|12=draft}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|12=draft}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=draft}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|12=draft}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shortest 100 pages]] [[Category:as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:Translated shrines and temples]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:王子神社]] [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]]

revision.text:
{{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} {{Shrine |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|12=draft}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|12=draft}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=draft}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|12=draft}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|12=draft}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|12=draft}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=draft}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=draft}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候|12=draft}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=draft}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=draft}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=draft}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=draft}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=draft}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=draft}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=draft}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=draft}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|12=draft}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=draft}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|12=draft}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|12=draft}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=draft}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|12=draft}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shortest 100 pages]] [[Category:as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:Translated shrines and temples]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:王子神社]] [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]]

The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候|12=draft}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=draft}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=draft}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=draft}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=draft}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=draft}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=draft}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=draft}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=draft}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|12=draft}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=draft}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|12=draft}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|12=draft}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=draft}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|12=draft}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shortest 100 pages]] [[Category:as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:Translated shrines and temples]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:王子神社]] [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]]

{{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} {{Shrine |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|12=draft}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|12=draft}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=draft}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|12=draft}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|12=draft}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|12=draft}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=draft}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=draft}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候|12=draft}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=draft}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=draft}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=draft}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=draft}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=draft}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=draft}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=draft}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=draft}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|12=draft}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=draft}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|12=draft}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|12=draft}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=draft}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|12=draft}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shortest 100 pages]] [[Category:as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:王子神社]] [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]]

{{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} {{Shrine |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|12=draft}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|12=draft}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=draft}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|12=draft}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|12=draft}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|12=draft}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=draft}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=draft}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候|12=draft}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=draft}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=draft}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=draft}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=draft}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=draft}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=draft}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=draft}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=draft}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|12=draft}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=draft}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|12=draft}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|12=draft}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=draft}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|12=draft}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shortest 100 pages]] [[Category:as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:王子神社]] [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]]

For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|12=draft}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|12=draft}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=draft}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|12=draft}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shortest 100 pages]] [[Category:as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:王子神社]] [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]]

{{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Shrine |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=draft|12=draft}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft|12=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft|12=draft}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候|12=draft|12=draft}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|12=draft|12=draft}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|12=draft|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft|12=draft}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|12=draft|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ|12=draft|12=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|12=draft|12=draft}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft|12=draft}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=draft|12=draft}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft|12=draft}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=draft|12=draft}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=draft|12=draft}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|12=draft|12=draft}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Shortest 100 pages]] [[Category:as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] }}

{{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Shrine |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former UnRanked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Shortest 100 pages]] [[Category:as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] }}

revision.text:
'''王子神社'''（おうじじんじゃ）<!--とは、主に熊野十二権現など[[九十九王子]]に関連する神社。代表格は[[浜王子]]からの勧請。-->は[[神社]]。
* [[王子神社 (東京都北区)]]（王子権現） - 東京都[[北区 (東京都)|北区]]に鎮座。[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[准勅祭社]]、[[東京十社]]。[[若一王子]]に由来。 * [[王子神社 (横浜市緑区)]] - 神奈川県[[横浜市]][[緑区 (横浜市)|緑区]]に鎮座。 * [[王子神社 (厚木市)]] - 神奈川県[[厚木市]][[王子 (厚木市)|王子]]に鎮座。 * [[王子神社 (掛川市)]] - 静岡県[[掛川市]][[山崎 (掛川市)|山崎]]に鎮座。若一王子に由来。 * [[王子神社 (掛川市倉真)]] - 静岡県掛川市[[倉真]]に鎮座。 * [[王子神社 (掛川市上内田)]] - 静岡県掛川市[[上内田]]に鎮座。 * [[王子神社 (南伊豆町)]] - 静岡県[[賀茂郡]][[南伊豆町]]に鎮座。 * [[王子神社 (亀岡市)]]（王子権現） - 京都府[[亀岡市]][[篠町]]王子に鎮座。若一王子に由来。 * [[王子神社 (神戸市灘区)]] - 兵庫県[[神戸市]][[灘区]]に鎮座。 * [[王子神社 (加西市)]] - 兵庫県[[加西市]]に鎮座。 * [[王子神社 (高松市)]] - 香川県[[高松市]]に鎮座。 * [[王子神社 (徳島市)]] - 徳島県[[徳島市]]に鎮座。 * [[王子神社 (石井町)]] - 徳島県[[名東郡]][[石井町]]に鎮座。 * [[王子神社 (西予市)]] - 愛媛県[[西予市]]に鎮座。 * [[王子神社 (佐伯市)]] - 大分県[[佐伯市]]に鎮座。
<!-- == 参考 == 「王子神社」という名前を含む神社。 * [[一王子神社]] - [[新潟県]][[新発田市]]に鎮座。 * [[二王子神社]] - 新潟県新発田市に鎮座。 * [[三王子神社]] - 新潟県新発田市に鎮座。 * [[四王子神社]] - [[熊本県]][[玉名郡]][[長洲町]]に鎮座。 * [[八王子神社 (八王子市)]] - 東京都[[八王子市]]に鎮座。 * [[八王子神社 (恵那市)]] - [[岐阜県]][[恵那市]]に鎮座。 * [[若一王子神社 (大町市)]] - [[長野県]][[大町市]]に鎮座。 * [[若一王子神社 (藍住町)]] - 徳島県[[板野郡]][[藍住町]]に鎮座。 * [[若王子神社]] - 兵庫県[[神戸市]][[北区 (神戸市)|北区]]に鎮座。 * [[久保王子神社]] - [[島根県]][[邑智郡]][[美郷町 (島根県)|美郷町]]に鎮座。 * [[堂山王子神社]] - [[福島県]][[田村市]]に鎮座。 * [[福王子神社]] - 京都府京都市[[右京区]]に鎮座。旧社格は[[村社]]。 --> == 関連項目 == * [[皇子神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] * [[若一王子神社]] * [[若一神社]] * [[王子神]] * [[王子村 (曖昧さ回避)]]

Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Shortest 100 pages]] [[Category:as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] }} {{translated page|ja|王子神社|version=103534550|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Shrine |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former UnRanked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City) }} {{translated page|ja|王子神社|version=103534550|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Shortest 100 pages]] [[Category:as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]]
