revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 王子神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[八万村|八万町]][[向寺山]]55 |位置 = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[アマツヒコネ|天津日子根命]] |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 猫神さん、應神さん |例祭 = 春大祭 5月5日、秋大祭 10月22日 |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|拝殿正面]] [[ファイル:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|「おうらまいり」]] '''王子神社'''（おうじじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[八万町]]にある[[神社]]である。 [[向寺山]]の[[徳島県文化の森総合公園]]内に鎮座し、「'''猫神さん'''」の愛称で親しまれている。また「應神（おおじん）さん」とも呼ばれる。 == 歴史 == 祭神である[[天照大神]]の第三皇子・天津日子根命（[[アマツヒコネ]]）は太古より統治の根子神（土着神）として奉斎され社殿裏は命の御陵と伝承されているが「徳島県神社誌」によると以前の社記には戦国時代夷山城主庄野和泉守が同城鎮護の神として伊勢の多度神社より勧請奉斎し、以来庶民鎮守の神として崇敬厚しとある『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603）。 社殿裏は命の御陵などと徳島県ではこのような捏造が横行している。神社名の「王子」は、天照大神の皇子（王子）に由来する尊称と伝えられる。学業成就・商売繁盛・をはじめとする開運の神様として知られており、[[蜂須賀氏|蜂須賀候]]の家老である長谷川奉行家において代々崇敬されたと云われている。 境内の石碑『王子神社縁起』には[[天照大神]]の第一皇子・天忍穂耳命（[[アメノオシホミミ]]）を祀る一王子祠が八万町福万谷に、第二皇子・天之菩卑能命（[[アメノホヒ]]）を祀る[[豊国神社 (小松島市)|御縣神社]]が小松島市に、第三皇子・天津日子根命（[[アマツヒコネ]]）が当社に、第四皇子・活津日子根命（[[イクツヒコネ]]）を祀る[[四王子神社 (徳島市)|四王子神社]]が八万町長谷に、第五皇子・熊野久須毘命（[[クマノクスビ]]）を祀る[[熊野神社 (徳島市)|熊野神社]]が丈六町に在ると記されている。 また八万村史には第二皇子は八万町柿谷の二王子祠に祀られ、一王子から四王子までが八万村に在ったとされる。山麓の反対側（西側）には朝宮神社（古くは旧八万村の氏神で御祭神は大靈女尊（[[天照大神]]）と[[月読命]]）が、南側には天王神社（祭神・[[素盞嗚尊]]）も鎮座する。また社殿裏には命の御陵が存在する{{Cite web|和書|url=https://museum.bunmori.tokushima.jp/hachiman2008/guide.pdf|title=八万町の昔を探そう|publisher=徳島県立文化の森総合公園|accessdate=2024-04-26}}。 == 阿波の猫騒動 == 昔、那賀郡加茂村（現阿南市）の庄屋の娘・お松は身に覚えのない罪で捕らえられ、処刑されることになった。お松は、自分に罪をかぶせた人に報復するようにと愛猫のお玉に言い聞かせ亡くなった。お玉はお松に罪をかぶせた人々を祟ったため、長谷川奉行がこれを鎮めるべくお松とお玉の霊を祭った。これがいつしか「心願成就の猫神さん」として捉えられるようになった。 == 祭神 == * [[アマツヒコネ|天津日子根命]] * お松大明神 * お玉大明神 == 関連項目 == 徳島市内にある[[天照大神]]の王子を祀る神社。 * 一王子神社 - 第一皇子・[[天之忍穂耳命]]を祀る。 * [[豊国神社 (小松島市)|御縣神社]] - 第二皇子・[[天之菩卑能命]]を祀る。[[豊国神社 (小松島市)|豊国神社]]境内社。 * [[四王子神社 (徳島市)|四王子神社]] - 第四皇子・[[活津日子根命]]を祀る。 * [[熊野神社 (徳島市)|熊野神社]] - 第五皇子・[[熊野久須毘命]]を祀る。 == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]前より[[徳島市営バス]]3番乗り場から「文化の森」行き、またはバイパス経由「市原」行きに乗り「文化の森」で下車、または⑱「しらさぎ台」行き、「一宮」行き、「天の原西（入田）」行きに乗車し「園瀬橋」下車･徒歩10分。「市原」行きは終点で下車、徒歩約20分。 * [[徳島バス]]4番乗り場から佐那河内線「仁井田西」行き又は「神山高校前（大木経由）」行きに乗り「園瀬橋」下車・徒歩10分。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]][[文化の森駅]]より文化の森駅東バス停から徳島バス「市原」行きに乗り「文化の森」で下車、または徒歩35分。 * [[徳島県庁]]より[[国道55号]]を南へ車で約15分。「文化の森」の駐車場を利用。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// 王子神社] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html 王子神社 阿波ナビ] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=徳島県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おうししんしや}} [[Category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:ネコの文化]]

{{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} {{Shrine |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City|qq=}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|qq=}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}} |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City|qq=}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|qq=}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|qq=}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka clan|qq=}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|qq=}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Station|qq=}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku Railway|qq=}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|qq=}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka no Mori Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi Line|qq=}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku Railway|qq=}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|qq=}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 55|qq=}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} [[ja:王子神社 (徳島市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]] {{translated page|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|version=100150795|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|qq=}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka clan|qq=}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|qq=}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Station|qq=}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku Railway|qq=}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|qq=}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka no Mori Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi Line|qq=}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku Railway|qq=}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|qq=}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 55|qq=}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} [[ja:王子神社 (徳島市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]] {{translated page|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|version=100150795|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} {{Shrine |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City|qq=}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|qq=}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}} |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City|qq=}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|qq=}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|qq=}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka clan|qq=}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|qq=}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Station|qq=}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku Railway|qq=}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|qq=}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka no Mori Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi Line|qq=}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku Railway|qq=}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|qq=}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 55|qq=}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} [[ja:王子神社 (徳島市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]] {{translated page|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|version=100150795|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|qq=}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka clan|qq=}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|qq=}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Station|qq=}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku Railway|qq=}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|qq=}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka no Mori Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi Line|qq=}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku Railway|qq=}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|qq=}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 55|qq=}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} [[ja:王子神社 (徳島市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]] {{translated page|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|version=100150795|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City|qq=}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|qq=}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}} |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City|qq=}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|qq=}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|qq=}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|lt_ja=蜂須賀候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka clan|qq=}}. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|クマノクスビ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|lt=Amatsuhikone|lt_ja=天津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|qq=}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}. == Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Station|qq=}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku Railway|qq=}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|qq=}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka no Mori Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi Line|qq=}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku Railway|qq=}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|qq=}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 55|qq=}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} [[ja:王子神社 (徳島市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]] {{translated page|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|version=100150795|comment=Imported full ja history}}

For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunka no Mori Station|ja|文化の森駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka no Mori Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi Line|qq=}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku Railway|qq=}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|qq=}}, drive south on {{ill|National Route 55|ja|国道55号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 55|qq=}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} [[ja:王子神社 (徳島市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]] {{translated page|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|version=100150795|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} {{Shrine |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|qq=}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|qq=}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|qq=}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]’s third prince, [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]], has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Hachisuka clan|Hachisuka Retainer]]. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Amenooshihomimi]], is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, [[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]], is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]], is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]], is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, [[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubikoto]], is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|qq=}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}. == Transportation == * From in front of [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] on [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]], take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|qq=}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From [[:en:Bunkanomori Station|Bunka no Mori Station]] on the [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway]], from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|qq=}}, drive south on [[:en:Japan National Route 55|National Route 55]] for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} [[ja:王子神社 (徳島市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]] {{translated page|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|version=100150795|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|qq=}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|qq=}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|qq=}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]’s third prince, [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]], has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Hachisuka clan|Hachisuka Retainer]]. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Amenooshihomimi]], is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, [[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]], is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]], is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]], is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, [[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubikoto]], is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|qq=}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}. == Transportation == * From in front of [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] on [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]], take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|qq=}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From [[:en:Bunkanomori Station|Bunka no Mori Station]] on the [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway]], from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|qq=}}, drive south on [[:en:Japan National Route 55|National Route 55]] for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} [[ja:王子神社 (徳島市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]] {{translated page|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|version=100150795|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Cats in popular culture]] [[Category:徳島市の神社]]

Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|qq=}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}. == Transportation == * From in front of [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] on [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]], take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|qq=}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|qq=}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}. == Transportation == * From in front of [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] on [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]], take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|qq=}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From [[:en:Bunkanomori Station|Bunka no Mori Station]] on the [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway]], from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|qq=}}, drive south on [[:en:Japan National Route 55|National Route 55]] for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} [[ja:王子神社 (徳島市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Cat Culture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]] {{translated page|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|version=100150795|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Cats in popular culture]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima, Tokushima]] {{Commons category|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}}

{{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} {{Shrine |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg|260px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|qq=}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = }} [[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]] '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|qq=}}. It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|qq=}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|qq=}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin. == History == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]’s third prince, [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]], has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603). It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Hachisuka clan|Hachisuka Retainer]]. The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Amenooshihomimi]], is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, [[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]], is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]], is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]], is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, [[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubikoto]], is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town. == The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment". == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|qq=}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|qq=}}. == Transportation == * From in front of [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] on [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]], take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|qq=}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From [[:en:Bunkanomori Station|Bunka no Mori Station]] on the [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway]], from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|qq=}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|qq=}}, drive south on [[:en:Japan National Route 55|National Route 55]] for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}} * [http://oujijinja.com// Oji Shrine] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002067.html Oji Shrine Awa Navi] {{Shinto stub}} {{Prefecture stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} [[ja:王子神社 (徳島市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:王子神社]] {{translated page|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|version=100150795|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Cats in popular culture]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima, Tokushima]] {{Commons category|Oji-jinja (Tokushima)}}
