{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg |Location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390463}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|1=Mukouji-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415925}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Hachiman-chō|WD=Q11390465}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.04105555555556|N|134.5257222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228}} }}
[[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]]
'''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|1=Hachiman-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390465}}.
It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|1=Tokushima Bunkanomori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489886}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|1=Mukouji-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415925}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin.
== History == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]’s third prince, [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]], has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people<ref name=":0">‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603)</ref>.
It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Hachisuka clan|Hachisuka Retainer]]<ref name=":0" />.
The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Amenooshihomimi]], is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, [[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]], is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]], is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]], is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, [[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubikoto]], is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town<ref name=":0" />.
== The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment"<ref name=":0" />.
== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame no Oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ|1=Kumanokusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11298796}}.
== Transportation == * From in front of [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] on [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]], take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島市交通局|1=Tokushima City Transportation Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489713}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From [[:en:Bunkanomori Station|Bunka no Mori Station]] on the [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway]], from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|1=Tokushima prefectural government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24896216}}, drive south on [[:en:Japan National Route 55|National Route 55]] for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”.

As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment"<ref name=":0" />.
== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame no Oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ|1=Kumanokusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11298796}}.
== Transportation == * From in front of [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] on [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]], take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島市交通局|1=Tokushima City Transportation Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489713}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From [[:en:Bunkanomori Station|Bunka no Mori Station]] on the [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway]], from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|1=Tokushima prefectural government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24896216}}, drive south on [[:en:Japan National Route 55|National Route 55]] for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oji-jinja (Tokushima)]][[ja:王子神社 (徳島市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oji-jinja (Tokushima)]][[ja:王子神社 (徳島市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oji-jinja (Tokushima)]][[ja:王子神社 (徳島市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11573002]] Wikidata name: Ōji Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Shrine (Tokushima City)|1=Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573002}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg |Location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390463}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|1=Mukouji-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415925}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Hachiman-chō|WD=Q11390465}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.04105555555556|N|134.5257222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228}} }}
[[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]]
'''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|1=Hachiman-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390465}}.
It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|1=Tokushima Bunkanomori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489886}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|1=Mukouji-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415925}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin.
== History == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]’s third prince, [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]], has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people<ref name=":0">‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603)</ref>.
It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Hachisuka clan|Hachisuka Retainer]]<ref name=":0" />.
The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Amenooshihomimi]], is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, [[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]], is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]], is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]], is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, [[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubikoto]], is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town<ref name=":0" />.
== The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment"<ref name=":0" />.
== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame no Oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ|1=Kumanokusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11298796}}.
== Transportation == * From in front of [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] on [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]], take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島市交通局|1=Tokushima City Transportation Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489713}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From [[:en:Bunkanomori Station|Bunka no Mori Station]] on the [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway]], from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|1=Tokushima prefectural government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24896216}}, drive south on [[:en:Japan National Route 55|National Route 55]] for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”.

'''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Tokushima (city)|en|Tokushima (city)|WD=Q207309|lt=Tokushima City}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|1=Hachiman-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390465}}.
It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|1=Tokushima Bunkanomori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489886}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|1=Mukouji-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415925}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin.
== History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people<ref name=":0">‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603)</ref>.
It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|en|Hachisuka clan|WD=Q2975261|lt=Hachisuka Retainer}}<ref name=":0" />.
The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenooshihomimi}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|Ame no Hohi|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame no Hohi}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumanokusubi|en|Kumanokusubi|WD=Q11298796|lt=Kumano Kusubikoto}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town<ref name=":0" />.
== The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment"<ref name=":0" />.
== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame no Oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ|1=Kumanokusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11298796}}.
== Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|WD=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島市交通局|1=Tokushima City Transportation Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489713}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunkanomori Station|en|Bunkanomori Station|WD=Q4997739|lt=Bunka no Mori Station}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|en|Mugi Line|WD=Q860478|lt=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=Shikoku Railway}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|1=Tokushima prefectural government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24896216}}, drive south on {{ill|Japan National Route 55|en|Japan National Route 55|WD=Q6157738|lt=National Route 55}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) |image = File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan04s5.jpg |Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Tokushima (city)|en|Tokushima (city)|WD=Q207309|lt=Tokushima City}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万村|lt=Yatsumachi|lt_ja=八万町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390463}} {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|1=Mukouji-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415925}} 55 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|27.8|N|134|31|32.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}} |Shrine Rank = Former Unranked Shrine |Foundation = |Main Hall = |Also Known As = Cat God, Ōjin |Festivals = Spring Grand Festival (May 5), Autumn Grand Festival (October 22) |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Hachiman-chō|WD=Q11390465}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.04105555555556|N|134.5257222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228}} }}
[[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]]
'''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Tokushima (city)|en|Tokushima (city)|WD=Q207309|lt=Tokushima City}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|1=Hachiman-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390465}}.
It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|1=Tokushima Bunkanomori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489886}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|1=Mukouji-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415925}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin.
== History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people<ref name=":0">‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603)</ref>.
It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|en|Hachisuka clan|WD=Q2975261|lt=Hachisuka Retainer}}<ref name=":0" />.
The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenooshihomimi}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|Ame no Hohi|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame no Hohi}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumanokusubi|en|Kumanokusubi|WD=Q11298796|lt=Kumano Kusubikoto}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town<ref name=":0" />.
== The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment"<ref name=":0" />.
== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame no Oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ|1=Kumanokusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11298796}}.
== Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|WD=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島市交通局|1=Tokushima City Transportation Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489713}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunkanomori Station|en|Bunkanomori Station|WD=Q4997739|lt=Bunka no Mori Station}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|en|Mugi Line|WD=Q860478|lt=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=Shikoku Railway}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|1=Tokushima prefectural government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24896216}}, drive south on {{ill|Japan National Route 55|en|Japan National Route 55|WD=Q6157738|lt=National Route 55}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”.

[[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]]
'''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Tokushima (city)|en|Tokushima (city)|WD=Q207309|lt=Tokushima City}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|1=Hachiman-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390465}}.
It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|1=Tokushima Bunkanomori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489886}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|1=Mukouji-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415925}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin.

However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people<ref name=":0">‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603)</ref>.
It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|en|Hachisuka clan|WD=Q2975261|lt=Hachisuka Retainer}}<ref name=":0" />.
The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenooshihomimi}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|Ame no Hohi|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame no Hohi}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumanokusubi|en|Kumanokusubi|WD=Q11298796|lt=Kumano Kusubikoto}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town<ref name=":0" />.
== The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment"<ref name=":0" />.
== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame no Oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ|1=Kumanokusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11298796}}.
== Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|WD=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島市交通局|1=Tokushima City Transportation Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489713}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunkanomori Station|en|Bunkanomori Station|WD=Q4997739|lt=Bunka no Mori Station}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|en|Mugi Line|WD=Q860478|lt=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=Shikoku Railway}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|1=Tokushima prefectural government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24896216}}, drive south on {{ill|Japan National Route 55|en|Japan National Route 55|WD=Q6157738|lt=National Route 55}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”.

[[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]]
'''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Tokushima (city)|en|Tokushima (city)|WD=Q207309|lt=Tokushima City}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|1=Hachiman-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390465}}.
It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|1=Tokushima Bunkanomori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489886}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|1=Mukouji-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415925}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin.
== History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people<ref name=":0">‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603)</ref>.
It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|en|Hachisuka clan|WD=Q2975261|lt=Hachisuka Retainer}}<ref name=":0" />.
The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenooshihomimi}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|Ame no Hohi|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame no Hohi}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumanokusubi|en|Kumanokusubi|WD=Q11298796|lt=Kumano Kusubikoto}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town<ref name=":0" />.
== The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment"<ref name=":0" />.
== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame no Oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ|1=Kumanokusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11298796}}.
== Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|WD=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島市交通局|1=Tokushima City Transportation Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489713}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunkanomori Station|en|Bunkanomori Station|WD=Q4997739|lt=Bunka no Mori Station}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|en|Mugi Line|WD=Q860478|lt=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=Shikoku Railway}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|1=Tokushima prefectural government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24896216}}, drive south on {{ill|Japan National Route 55|en|Japan National Route 55|WD=Q6157738|lt=National Route 55}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”.

It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|1=Tokushima Bunkanomori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489886}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|1=Mukouji-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415925}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin.
== History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people<ref name=":0">‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603)</ref>.
It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|en|Hachisuka clan|WD=Q2975261|lt=Hachisuka Retainer}}<ref name=":0" />.
The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenooshihomimi}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|Ame no Hohi|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame no Hohi}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumanokusubi|en|Kumanokusubi|WD=Q11298796|lt=Kumano Kusubikoto}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town<ref name=":0" />.
== The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment"<ref name=":0" />.
== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame no Oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ|1=Kumanokusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11298796}}.
== Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|WD=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島市交通局|1=Tokushima City Transportation Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489713}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunkanomori Station|en|Bunkanomori Station|WD=Q4997739|lt=Bunka no Mori Station}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|en|Mugi Line|WD=Q860478|lt=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=Shikoku Railway}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|1=Tokushima prefectural government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24896216}}, drive south on {{ill|Japan National Route 55|en|Japan National Route 55|WD=Q6157738|lt=National Route 55}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”.
== References == <references />

[[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]]
'''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Tokushima (city)|en|Tokushima (city)|WD=Q207309|lt=Tokushima City|1=Tokushima (city)}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|1=Hachiman-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390465}}.
It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|1=Tokushima Bunkanomori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489886}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|1=Q11415925|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415925}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin.
== History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone|1=Amatsuhikone}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people<ref name=":0">‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603)</ref>.
It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|en|Hachisuka clan|WD=Q2975261|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|1=Hachisuka clan}}<ref name=":0" />.
The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenooshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|Ame no Hohi|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame no Hohi|1=Ame no Hohi}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone|1=Amatsuhikone}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone|1=Ikutsuhikone}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumanokusubi|en|Kumanokusubi|WD=Q11298796|lt=Kumano Kusubikoto|1=Kumanokusubi}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town<ref name=":0" />.
== The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment"<ref name=":0" />.
== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone|1=Amatsuhikone}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ|1=Kumanokusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11298796}}.
== Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|WD=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station|1=Tokushima Station}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島市交通局|1=Tokushima City Transportation Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489713}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunkanomori Station|en|Bunkanomori Station|WD=Q4997739|lt=Bunka no Mori Station|1=Bunkanomori Station}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|en|Mugi Line|WD=Q860478|lt=Mugi Line|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=Shikoku Railway|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|1=Tokushima prefectural government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24896216}}, drive south on {{ill|Japan National Route 55|en|Japan National Route 55|WD=Q6157738|lt=National Route 55|1=Japan National Route 55}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”.

[[File:Ohji-jinja in Tokushima City.jpg|thumb|Front view of the Haiden]] [[File:Oji-jinja Tokushima Japan06s5.jpg|thumb|"Oura Pilgrimage"]]
'''Oji Shrine''' (王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Tokushima (city)|en|Tokushima (city)|WD=Q207309|lt=Tokushima City|1=Tokushima (city)}} {{ill|Yatsumachi|ja|八万町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsumachi|1=Hachiman-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390465}}.
It is situated within {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|ja|徳島県文化の森総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture Cultural Forest Park|1=Tokushima Bunkanomori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489886}} on {{ill|Mukouji-yama|ja|向寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukouji-yama|1=Q11415925|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415925}}, and is affectionately known as '''Cat God'''. It is also called Ōjin.
== History == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}’s third prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone|1=Amatsuhikone}}, has been worshipped as the foundational local god since ancient times, and it is traditionally believed that his mausoleum lies behind the shrine’s main hall. However, according to the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Chronicles, earlier shrine records state that during the Sengoku period, the lord of Iya Castle, Shono Izuminokami, enshrined him as the guardian deity of the castle by inviting him from Ise’s Tato Shrine, and since then he has been deeply revered as the protector of the common people<ref name=":0">‘{{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture’ (1986, ISBN 4040013603)</ref>.
It is said that in Tokushima Prefecture, claims such as the shrine’s rear housing his mausoleum are rampant. The shrine’s name, “Oji,” is believed to be an honorific derived from the prince of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, and the deity is renowned as a god of good fortune for academic success, business prosperity, and overall luck. He was venerated for generations by the Hasegawa Bugyō family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|en|Hachisuka clan|WD=Q2975261|lt=Hachisuka Retainer|1=Hachisuka clan}}<ref name=":0" />.
The stone monument “Oji Shrine Origins” on the shrine grounds records that: * The First Prince, {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenooshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, is enshrined at the First Prince Shrine in Fukumaya, Yatsumachi; * The Second Prince, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|Ame no Hohi|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame no Hohi|1=Ame no Hohi}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} in Komatsushima City; * The Third Prince, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone|1=Amatsuhikone}}, is enshrined in this shrine; * The Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone|1=Ikutsuhikone}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} in Hase, Yatsumachi; * The Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumanokusubi|en|Kumanokusubi|WD=Q11298796|lt=Kumano Kusubikoto|1=Kumanokusubi}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} in Juroku Town<ref name=":0" />.
== The Awa Cat Incident == Long ago, O‐Matsu, the daughter of the headman of Kamo Village in Naka District (present-day Anan City), was captured and condemned to execution for a crime she did not commit. Before her death, she urged her beloved cat, Otama, to exact vengeance on those who falsely accused her. As a result of Otama’s curse upon these individuals, Hasegawa Bugyō enshrined the spirits of O‐Matsu and Otama to quell the disturbance. Over time, this came to be regarded as the "Cat God of Wish Fulfillment"<ref name=":0" />.
== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone|1=Amatsuhikone}} * O‐Matsu Daimyojin (お松大明神) * Otama Daimyojin (お玉大明神) == See Also == In Tokushima City, there are shrines enshrining the princes of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}. * First Prince Shrine – enshrines the First Prince, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}. * {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Second Prince, {{ill|Amenohobihinomikoto|ja|天之菩卑能命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohobihinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}, and is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Toyokuni Shrine|ja|豊国神社 (小松島市)|lt=Mikata Shrine|lt_ja=豊国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokuni Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shioji Shrine|ja|四王子神社 (徳島市)|lt=Shioji Shrine|lt_ja=四王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioji Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fourth Prince, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}. * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines the Fifth Prince, {{ill|Kumano Kusubikoto|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ|1=Kumanokusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11298796}}.
== Transportation == * From in front of {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|WD=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station|1=Tokushima Station}} on {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}, take {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島市交通局|1=Tokushima City Transportation Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489713}} from stop 3 for “Bunka no Mori” or, via the bypass, for “Ichihara”, and alight at “Bunka no Mori”; alternatively, board routes ⑱ for “Shirasagidai”, “Ichinomiya”, or “Amanohara Nishi (Iritada)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (a 10‐minute walk). For the “Ichihara” route, disembark at the terminal stop and walk about 20 minutes. * From stop 4 of {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}}, board the Sanakouchi Line for “Niida Nishi” or for “Kamiyama High School Front (via Oki)” and alight at “Sonosebashi” (10‐minute walk). * From {{ill|Bunkanomori Station|en|Bunkanomori Station|WD=Q4997739|lt=Bunka no Mori Station|1=Bunkanomori Station}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|en|Mugi Line|WD=Q860478|lt=Mugi Line|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=Shikoku Railway|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}, from the station’s east bus stop, take {{ill|Tokushima Bus|ja|徳島バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Bus|1=Tokushima Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489648}} for “Ichihara” and alight at “Bunka no Mori”, or walk 35 minutes. * From {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Office|ja|徳島県庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Office|1=Tokushima prefectural government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24896216}}, drive south on {{ill|Japan National Route 55|en|Japan National Route 55|WD=Q6157738|lt=National Route 55|1=Japan National Route 55}} for about 15 minutes and use the parking lot at “Bunka no Mori”.
